How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-20 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers where two or more coherent screeners agree within one trading day, with validated combos ranked first by historical edge.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PANW is the outlier with a score of 1253 across four screeners - CLENOW_MOMENTUM, CS_RANKER, PULLBACK, and VOLUME_LEADER - separating itself from every other name in the batch by a factor of more than two. AMAT follows at 489 with three screeners, sharing the same CLENOW, CS_RANKER, PULLBACK stack as CSCO at 304, suggesting tech and semi momentum is the dominant theme driving overlap today. No ticker carries a matched_combo, meaning none of the active screeners include SMART_FLOW or UNUSUAL_V3 and none fire a VALID validated combination - the batch runs on raw overlap count, not on the tested combos with the strongest backtest credentials. Score distribution across the 20 overlaps is severely top-heavy: PANW at 1253 is a lone outlier, the remaining nine names compress between 99 and 489, and score_stats beyond overlap count are not provided. NEUTRAL macro at 52.95 is not a headwind but offers no lift, so take PANW first at moderate conviction on score dominance and four-screener breadth, sized smaller than you would if a VALID combo had fired.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: PYPL (score 7, target ~$54.44 (DD20d -4.3%)).

2 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.
Filter

All Screeners · ranked by backtest quality · type in the search box or click a chip to filter

🧠 Flags unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta (open vs close), filtered through EMA8/21 alignment and low-OI anomaly to isolate informed flow.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $XYZ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1XYZ ↑50 Watch$80.62 +1.68%BULLISH SWEEPCALL83$240KOPENING102858STRONG22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With only 1 resolved trade out of 256 total and the monthly cohorts (April through June all printing 100% win rates) composed almost entirely of unrealized, still-open positions (0% expired), the live tape cannot confirm or contradict the backtest's 68.1% win rate and 2.39 Sharpe, and the only number that matters right now is 1.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 256 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name, XYZ, a score-50 Watch-tier bullish sweep with zero signals at or above 65 and zero above 80. The score distribution is not a distribution, it is one datapoint sitting at the median floor, which means the screener found nothing with real conviction. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 52.95) neither blocks nor supports a long, but it provides no tailwind to override a weak signal. Pass on XYZ at this score, or size to minimal if the sweep confirmation looked clean intraday, and wait for a batch where at least one name clears 65.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the backtest - live Signal tier win rate of 79.4% and average of +3.79% per trade sit close to the OOS-robust Sharpe 2.34, and the monthly cohort trend reinforces this direction, with win rate climbing from 50.8% in April to 77.2% in June, the key number being that +26-point recovery in three months.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DUK and KMB lead at score 77 (Signal tier), with EXC at 76 close behind, all three benefiting from the screener's Sinclair utility sector adjustment of +31%. Six of the eight top picks are utilities or regulated infrastructure, which is consistent with construction but compresses dispersion and leaves the batch sector-concentrated rather than broad. All 34 signals fall between 65 and 77, median 70, with zero names clearing 80 - a tight mid-range cluster with no standout high-conviction name. Pattern data is null across all picks, so there is no supplementary signal overlay to sharpen entries. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 52.95) does not block variance premium selling but offers no directional support; DUK is the first name at moderate conviction, highest score and cleanest sector fit.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

COLO at a perfect 100 and CMG at 99 lead today, both Very Strong tier, with no pattern data available to add setup color on either name. CCJ at 95 is the third name worth watching, the uranium angle making it the most differentiated pick in a batch that otherwise tilts toward consumer and cyclical names. Score distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals score 80 or above, median at 75, meaning real density at the high end rather than a scattered field. The macro regime lands at NEUTRAL with a score of 52.95, which neither amplifies nor undermines this batch, as the VRP edge is structural and regime-agnostic. COLO is the first name at highest conviction given the perfect score, but absent pattern confirmation, size conservatively.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

ADMA and CCJ both print perfect 100s at Very strong tier, the two cleanest reads in today's batch and the clearest expressions of the Kerberos setup firing. EWT comes in at 79, Signal tier, worth monitoring but a clear step below the leaders in conviction. Five signals total, median 79, two at or above 80 - the distribution is top-heavy, with a real quality gap between the top two and the tail, where SANM at 67 and SA at 61 are marginal. Neutral macro at 52.95 is neither a headwind nor a tailwind for a contrarian put-vol strategy that does not depend on directional regime alignment to work. Start with ADMA or CCJ at high conviction, no preference between them on the available data since both score identically and pattern fields are unpopulated.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape contradicts the backtest cleanly - 46% win rate versus the backtested 65%, avg return -3.5% against SPY at -0.6%, a -2.9 point gap that justifies the KILL verdict - while the June 2026 monthly cohort (60% WR, +2.1% avg) is the sole recent data point hinting at stabilisation, not confirming a turn.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 46% · avg -3.5% vs SPY -0.6% (-2.9 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 492 resolved at +20d · WR 46% · avg -3.5% · vs SPY -0.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 495 closed · WR TB 37% · 16% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ARKO, H, MRTN, RLJ, and SGC all hit the 95-score ceiling with Very strong tier LAUNCH patterns, joined by AIN, APLE, and CNOB at 90. Every one of today's 50 signals scores at or above 80 with a median of 85, compressing the distribution into a single cluster that eliminates any within-batch ranking. NEUTRAL macro at 52.95 provides no regime tailwind for a reversal play, so the screener is running on technical signal alone. The critical overlay is that the Very strong tier in live 90-day tracking posted just a 38% win rate and -6.7% avg return - the worst of any tier - which directly undercuts today's entire top slice, so if you trade any name here, size down to half-kelly and require confirmed intraday follow-through before entry.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Five tickers tie at the 94-score ceiling - LUMN, QBTS, SMR, BTQ, and LAES - all Very Strong tier, all LONG direction, and none carrying pattern confirmation, which limits differentiation to external judgment. LUMN is the most straightforward LEAPS candidate on liquidity grounds, and QBTS and SMR add speculative beta for a basket that benefits from strict position sizing discipline. The score distribution is unusually compressed: all 25 signals print 80 or above, median sits at 87, with the full batch clustering within 7 points of the 94 ceiling - top-heavy and tight, not broad or dispersed, signaling a structurally thin day rather than a rich opportunity set. NEUTRAL macro at 52.95 is neither obstacle nor accelerant for the 120-day reversion window, consistent with a tape that does not actively punish or reward contrarian entries. Lead with LUMN as basket anchor at 1-2% per the backtest's own sizing parameters, and treat the null pattern flag across all names as a hard cap on conviction.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, and calendar effects to flag optimal sell-vol entry points.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑64 Watch$747.68 +0.75%SELL VOL1.64
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Captures informed options flow where volume tops 5x the 20-day mean, premium clears $50K, and Vol/OI exceeds 5, then filters out extended and volatile tickers.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Monthly cohorts from March through June each post 100% win rates across 184 to 676 signals, consistent with the 90-day tracker's 100% on 15 resolved, but with only 15 resolved out of 620 flagged, sample-size limits mean neither confirms nor refutes the backtest's MARGINAL call - the key number is 15, well short of the 20-trade minimum.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HIMS at 87 (Strong, bullish sweep) is the only name above 80 and the one clean directional long in the batch. CIFR (79, bearish sweep tagged LONG) and AFRM (72, same pattern) look more like protective put flow than upside conviction. Score distribution is thin: median 59, only five names at or above 65, with the bulk of the eight-name batch clustered in Watch territory. The neutral macro regime (score 52.95) fits the hedging character of this batch, where three of the top five signals are bearish sweeps. HIMS first at medium conviction - it is the lone clean signal - but keep sizing light given a batch this compressed.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLP scores 95 (Very Strong) and is the cleanest name in today's batch, with XLK at 80 (Strong) the only other actionable long clearly above the midfield. Four of 11 signals reach 80 or better, but n_ge_65 matches n_ge_80 exactly, confirming a hard gap between the top cluster and the 55-or-below mass with nothing scoring between 65 and 79. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (52.95), which is not a tailwind for sector rotation calls, and pattern classifications are null across every pick, stripping out the lifecycle context the screener is built around. Take XLP first at reduced size given the NEUTRAL backdrop and absent pattern data; pass on XLK and the 55-score cluster until the regime firms.

🧠 Screens for deeply oversold stocks showing capitulation volume and a reversal candle, exploiting mean reversion after forced selling exhausts supply.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the alpha, with avg +3.3% beating SPY by 3.9 points close to the 3.5% backtest mean pnl, but the live 56.2% win rate trails the 63.3% backtest figure and the monthly trend from January's 29.3% cohort win rate to June's 56.0% makes clear this screener is sensitive to macro regime, not structurally robust across all tapes.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

RGC (85) and JFIN (81) sit in the Strong tier, but tracking data for that tier shows a 37.9% win rate and a -3.0% average return, worse than the Signal names below them. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 52.95) provides no directional lift for a bounce play. Start with LVWR at moderate conviction and skip the Strong tier names entirely until their tier-level tracking improves.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores stocks on annualized 90-day regression slope times R-squared, filtered above MA100, with a SPY-above-MA200 regime gate.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Price ≥ $5, volume ≥ 500K, market cap ≥ $500M
  • Fixed 5-day hold
Score
min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Four names print a score of 100, GS, PLD, TRV, and UNH, all in the Very strong tier, but that tier is where live performance collapses most visibly: 30% win rate and a negative 11.9% average 20-day return in tracking, making the perfect score a yellow flag rather than a buy signal. MS at 72 and WFC at 66 sit in the Signal tier, which carries a 66.7% win rate and a positive 2.6% average return in the live sample, so these are the more credible setups on today's card. The 8-name batch is top-heavy with half the names at max score and a median of 72, but all patterns are null, meaning the score reflects momentum and volume mechanics alone with no structural setup to anchor the trade. Macro sits at NEUTRAL with a score of 52.95, which neither supports nor fights the trade, but the June cohort printing 0 wins and a negative 20.4% average 20-day return is a fresh drag that sits inside the 90-day window and deserves weight. First preference is MS at low conviction, ahead of the perfect-scorers, given the tier inversion visible in the live data.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more intermarket correlation pairs break past two sigma over seven days, betting on mean reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Flags post-earnings gap continuations: positive EPS surprise plus gap-up that holds, scored on surprise size, gap magnitude, post-gap action, and analyst revisions.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $AEHR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1AEHR ↑78● Signal$96.67 +24.95%1566.7%27.4%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape supports the KEEP verdict at 55.3% WR and avg +3.46% beating SPY's +0.37% by 3.1 points, but the June 2026 cohort cracks the story - 37.2% WR and -0.65% avg on 145 names is a sharp reversal from May's 59.8% WR, suggesting the recent tape is deteriorating rather than confirming the aggregate.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PATH scores a perfect 100 (Very Strong long) and leads a tight top cluster: MAX at 96 and HL at 95 are the next two names, all three confirmed as fresh positions by OI delta with no pattern qualifier limiting the read. The score distribution is top-heavy but broadly populated: all 50 signals clear 65, 19 clear 80, median sits at 76, so there is real depth below the leaders without a long tail of junk. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 52.95) provides no directional amplification, meaning these reads stand on flow alone, not on a macro tailwind. PATH is the first choice at highest conviction; size the rest of the Very Strong cluster smaller given the neutral tape, and skip the Strong names unless conviction builds intraday.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.

AEIS and CAMT are the cleanest single-stock names in the batch, both scoring 100 in the Very strong tier alongside an ETF bloc of ARKK, ASHR, BLOK, BRZU, BUZZ, and AMZU. Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 58%, avg +2.6% versus SPY +0.05%) supports the KEEP verdict and the May and June cohort averages above +4% confirm a positive trend after April's -0.73% drag, but the key number is the tier split: Strong names produced WR 69% and avg +7.8% while Very Strong names ran WR just 12.5% and avg -13.2%, making today's all-Strong batch the correct tier to be in.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AKR and BV lead with tied max scores of 85, Strong tier and PRIMED, the only two names at the ceiling of today's 50-signal universe. ABX sits next at 80, also Strong and PRIMED, giving a credible three-name shortlist. Score distribution is moderately concentrated: 12 of 50 signals score 80 or above with the median at 70, so the top cluster is real without being extremely thin. NEUTRAL macro at 52.95 provides no directional tailwind for a screener that depends on forced covering, which limits conviction. AKR first, at moderate conviction only, given the max score and the reality that this screener carries no rigorous backtest behind it.

🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume times multi-horizon momentum to surface volume-led breakouts; filters require positive 20-day momentum and price above $5.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals today score 96 or above, with DINO, DPZ, and NU each printing 100 - the ceiling - while CFR, EFX, GPN, HUBS, and HXL sit at 99. The distribution is maximally compressed: every signal clears the 80-score threshold and the median lands at 96, meaning there is no meaningful spread within today's batch to rank by. Pattern data is null across all names, leaving raw score as the only differentiator among names that are otherwise indistinguishable on this screen. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.95 offers no directional amplifier for momentum names, so the setup is not regime-hostile but not regime-supported either. Given the KILL verdict on live tracking, conviction is low; if forced to choose, DINO and NU as the two top-scored names with sector diversity would go first, at minimal size.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Flags rejection-tail bars at value-area extremes using Market Profile, capturing mean reversion where price is structurally rejected from institutional acceptance zones.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $RPHS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1RPHS ↑90●●● Very strong$8.79 -0.28%LOW1.8126.8%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With only 9 resolved trades the live tape (55.6% WR, avg +1.36% vs SPY -0.04%) sits directionally in line with the backtest filter result of +3.18% at 10 days, but the June 2026 cohort's collapse to 50% WR and 0.11% average from May's 66.7% and 3.85% is a yellow flag, and no number here clears the 20-trade minimum the system needs before the WATCH verdict upgrades.

🧠 Catches PEAD continuation entries: weekly red-candle pullback within four weeks of an earnings gap-up, then a weekly close above the prior week's high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the formal rejection: a 49% win rate and -3.8% average against SPY's -0.6% produces a 3.2-point shortfall earning the KILL verdict, and the June 2026 cohort at 45.9% WR across 4,139 signals - the largest and most recent data point - shows decay accelerating rather than mean-reverting toward edge.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

APGE and ATAI both hit the score ceiling at 80 with TRIGGER tags, the strongest read this screener can produce. The catch: Strong-tier history across 90 days covers only 2 trades with a 50% win rate and a -29.83% average return, meaning prior high-conviction calls were net destroyers, and today adds 2 more to that same thin bucket. CRNX, MAN, and RPD at 75 in the SOW ZONE sit in the Signal tier, which runs a 35.9% win rate and -7.46% average across 64 trades, worse than the Watch tier on both metrics. Score distribution clusters around the 60 median, with 24 of 50 names at or above 65 but only 2 at the ceiling - broad in the middle, thin at the top, not a setup that screams concentrated edge. The NEUTRAL macro at 52.95 provides no directional tailwind for shorts; if this screener must be acted on, APGE first over ATAI by coin-flip logic only, minimum size, defined risk via puts, conviction low.

🧠 Flags stocks where weekly RSI(14) crosses below 30, then daily candlestick and zone scoring confirm a reversal setup targeting oversold mean reversion.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d live record partially rehabilitates the doctrine-rejected backtest, posting WR 53.4% and avg +3.65% against SPY +0.91%, but monthly cohorts show clear deceleration from March's 78.8% WR and +5.9% avg down to May's negative avg of -0.44% and June's 52.6% WR, which is the key number that suggests the edge is compressing as the tape normalizes.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

OR at 70 and AIV at 65 are the only two names clearing the Signal threshold today, both in SETUP pattern, which gives them the only credible entries in this batch. The remaining six names cluster between 55 and 61 with nothing above 70 and zero names reaching 80, making the score distribution compressed and thin rather than top-heavy or broadly strong. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.95 does not block contrarian bottom-fishing but offers no directional tailwind to accelerate mean reversion. Take OR first at low-to-moderate conviction given it holds the top score; AIV is the follow-on watch if OR confirms intraday.

🧠 Detects unusual volume and OI delta in crypto-linked ETFs versus their rolling mean, weighted by flow intensity and spot crypto correlation to flag directional conviction.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $CORZ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1CORZ ↑50 Watch$24.16 +7.86%41.93-23.6%risk_on
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IPAY leads at 75, the only name with meaningful separation from the pack, followed by HACK at 71 - fintech and cybersecurity are at least coherent theme candidates. The score distribution is weak: nothing clears 80, only 6 of 50 signals reach 65, and the median sits at exactly 50, meaning the universe is clustered around a noise-level midpoint. The presence of VNQ, XLP, and XOP in a themes screener is a construction red flag - those are broad sector ETFs, not theme plays, which suggests the holdings filter is bleeding in off-mandate names. Macro is NEUTRAL at 52.95, no directional tailwind. If you act at all, IPAY first at low conviction, no pattern field to sharpen entry.

FINS, JEF, and LNBIX each score 100 with a Very Strong tier, while SVRE and TYG land at 99, but no pattern data is attached to any name so differentiation stops at rank and purchase size. The score distribution is a ceiling-effect problem: all 50 signals score at or above 80 and the median sits at 94, meaning the screener is not separating today's batch in any useful way. A NEUTRAL macro regime (52.95) adds no sector or momentum tailwind to lean on. With liquidity and analyst coverage making insider moves easiest to cross-reference and trade, JEF gets the first look, but conviction is moderate at best given the absence of pattern context across the entire list.

🧠 The screener has no backtest to validate against, so the 90d live record does all the work: 65% WR and +7.9% avg beating SPY by 8.8 pts earns a KEEP verdict, and the lone May cohort's 66% WR broadly confirms that direction, though its 43% average mean is a one-outlier artifact from a +1,385% best print.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 65% · avg +7.9% · beats SPY -0.9% by +8.8 pts
Last 90 days: 136 signals captured · 131 resolved at +20d · WR 65% · avg +7.9% · vs SPY -0.9%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 37% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight tickers score a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier: BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM, all flagged LONG. SRTY is a 3x inverse Russell ETF, and its presence alongside defensive dividend name HDV signals the screener is purely technical and sector-blind. Score distribution runs top-heavy: 41 of 50 signals print above 65 and 12 above 80, with a median of 70. Macro is NEUTRAL at 52.95, no tailwind or headwind to factor in. Pattern data is null across the board, so the eight names are effectively tied on available metrics; in a NEUTRAL tape with no pattern differentiation, HDV is the lowest-risk entry in the batch, first preference at low conviction only.

🧠 Detects institutional accumulation by scoring quarterly 13F position changes from 20 top hedge funds, rewarding new entries and sizing increases with a structured point system.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

META and ASML lead the eight-name shortlist at Very Strong tier and 100 scores: META for AI monetization momentum and ASML as the sole EUV chokepoint globally. SPOT, TPG, and MKSI round out a batch spanning streaming, alternative assets, and semiconductor equipment, signaling whale interest is not concentrated in one sector. All 50 signals printed at the 100-point ceiling with a median of 100, making score-based differentiation impossible this quarter. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.95 does not confirm or amplify the setup, so this reads as a pure positioning signal with no macro tailwind. Take META first at moderate conviction for liquidity and catalyst density, with ASML as the next look if semis macro firms.

🧠 Ranks US equities by EV/EBIT ascending and bets that the cheapest operating-earnings names revert to fair value as the market reprices neglected assets.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows a 57% win rate and a +5.1% average 20-day return against SPY's +0.6%, a +4.5-point edge that earns a KEEP verdict, but with backtest_validity returning NOT_YET_BACKTESTED and only one monthly cohort available, the live results have no statistical floor to confirm or contradict - the +4.5-point number is promising and not yet provable.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ tops the batch at a perfect score of 100 in the Very strong tier, making it the cleanest Acquirer's Multiple expression in the universe today. WRD at 97 and KROS at 93, both Very strong, round out a credible three-name core with no pattern catalyst needed given the score separation. The distribution is broad rather than clustered, with 7 of 16 names above 80 and a median of 73, so this is not a single-name day. NEUTRAL macro at 52.95 neither amplifies nor undercuts a value rotation thesis, and the Strong-tier names (GPC, PRE, NVAX) carry a caveat since that tier has printed negative average returns in the 90-day live track. BZ is the first name at high conviction, WRD second.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Finds individual stocks lagging within sectors already in LATE_CHASE or RIDING phase, scoring each name by sector relative strength multiplied by the stock's underperformance gap.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

The names split loosely into mortgage REITs (PMT, BXMT, ABR, FRMI) and uranium equities (NXE, CCJ, UEC), with CSGP as the lone real-estate data outlier, but every ticker carries a null pattern and Watch-tier conviction, so no name has a structural edge over the others. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (52.95), a regime that does not supply the directional tailwind catch-up compression strategies depend on. Pass on the full batch today, no first look warranted at any name.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Flags coordinated insider accumulation via SEC Form 4 clusters, where 3+ insiders buying within 30 days signals conviction outside investors cannot see.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.
Top picks: $CTM $FNWD $GLOO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayBuysCXOClusterAvg value
1CTM ↑60 Watch$0.62 +1.33%4✓✓$1K
2FNWD ↑60 Watch$36.29 -0.14%6✓✓$1K
3GLOO ↑60 Watch$3.27 +0.31%4✓✓$2250K
4SWZ ↑60 Watch$5.94 +0.13%3✓✓$30K
5TSM ↑60 Watch$419.49 +4.27%34✓✓$15K
6WBX ↑60 Watch$4.56 -3.18%4✓✓$1245K
7YORW ↑60 Watch$30.67 -1.89%7✓✓$2K
8KMX ↑55 Watch$57.56 +2.95%3·✓$213K
9COE ↑50 Watch$15.98 -2.02%5✓·$14451K
10LILA ↑50 Watch$7.25 -1.36%3✓·$1500K
11PRQR ↑50 Watch$1.84 +4.83%4✓·$247K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (WR 62%, avg +5.4%, beating SPY by 5.8 points, KEEP verdict) and the May cohort (64% WR, +4.9%) confirm directional usefulness, but April's 49% WR with a 539% best-name inflating the cohort average signals outlier dependence, and there is no rigorous backtest to anchor confidence, so the key number to watch is whether June's 59% WR holds as more positions resolve.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies the highest relative-strength tickers inside bullish sectors by combining 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank into a single score.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $ABX $ACA $ACAD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$10.38 +1.22%Financials18.7%30.6%
2ACA ↑68● Signal$144.76 +0.01%Industrials12.2%27.2%
3ACAD ↑64 Watch$25.11 +0.06%Healthcare18.9%13.9%
4ABVX ↑58 Watch$133.22 +1.31%Healthcare40.6%7.9%
5ACCO ↑57 Watch$4.00 -0.87%Industrials-2.5%25.5%
6AAMI ↑55 Watch$83.92 +2.15%Financials0.8%21.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (57% WR, avg +4.4%, beating SPY by 6.1 points on 180 resolved trades) confirms the screener earns its KEEP verdict, but the Signal tier at 73.2% WR and +9.4% avg is the load-bearing number - the May cohort's +10.8% avg is distorted by a single +1149% outlier and should not be read as confirmation of trend momentum.

IV/HV Fear Spread

sizing guide, not permission
90d tape
HALF SIZE
WR 42%
Refresh
EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 exceeds HV20 by more than 1.5 standard deviations, signaling elevated fear premium available to options sellers.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $MA $BMRN $IWM
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1MA ↓87●● Strong$541.47 -1.09%52%24%2.20EXTREME_FEAR
2BMRN ↓79● Signal$59.33 +0.37%46%23%2.00FEAR
3IWM ↓67● Signal$295.26 +1.01%31%18%1.69FEAR
4LLY ↓63 Watch$1162.97 +1.40%50%31%1.59FEAR
5BNTX ↓60 Watch$92.84 +0.74%44%29%1.52FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 There is no validated backtest to compare against (insufficient N at 25 resolved trades), so the 90d win rate of 42% and average return of +0.9% versus SPY at +1.1% stand on their own, and the monthly cohort trend is moving the wrong way - June 2026 printed a 30% win rate and -3.8% average after April ran at 55.9% and +10.8%, the key number being that three-month deterioration in average return from +10.8% to -3.8%.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breakouts above the Value Area High that close back inside, trapping longs and generating a short entry signal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AMBP $ABCL $ARCC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AMBP ↓80●● Strong$4.68 +3.97%BULL_TRAP4.733.99
2ABCL ↓65● Signal$5.99 +3.28%BULL_TRAP6.624.09
3ARCC ↓55 Watch$19.11 +0.71%BULL_TRAP19.2618.68
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KILL, not contradicts it: 53% win rate and average P&L of -1.4% versus SPY's -0.4% produces just 1 point of alpha, and the options overlay at 27% win rate on 78 trades is the single most damning number in this dataset.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices the expected move implied by Yang-Zhang 20-day historical volatility, surfacing cheap long-vol setups.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $DHR $IBM
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1DHR ↑50 Watch$173.34 -13.81%REVERSAL0UP (reversal)BUY CALL (reversal)
2IBM ↑50 Watch$210.43 -1.21%REVERSAL1UP (reversal)BUY CALL (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

XOM is the only name at Signal tier with a score of 75, making it the lone candidate worth any attention today. ROKU sits at Watch with a score of 50, which is a placeholder, not a trade. The batch is as thin as it gets: two signals, nothing above 80, median at 50. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 52.95 neither supports nor kills a long-vol thesis, but it provides no edge boost either. If forced to act, XOM first, small size, with the caveat that the absence of a pattern tag and a sub-80 score keeps this low-conviction.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags retail euphoria via social z-score, call/put spike, and price stretch with no earnings catalyst, positioning short against crowded retail momentum.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $CBRL $AMBA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1CBRL ↓87●● Strong$51.87 -2.88%EUPHORIA0.87-0.4%
2AMBA ↓68● Signal$69.25 +5.40%GREED0.696.2%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

🧠 The 90-day tape across 154 resolved trades delivers 55% win rate but a rounding-error edge of +0.0 points over SPY, the monthly trend from April through June is too volatile to build on with June falling to 46.7% win rate, and the formal backtest remains void at N=17, so the KILL verdict stands as the only defensible read on signal quality.

🧠 Composite vol screener combining IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and flow signals to flag options mispricings.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $UVXY $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1UVXY ↓85●● Strong$23.53 -4.93%81%861%RULE_1
2VXX ↓85●● Strong$21.39 -3.32%55%443%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Flags stocks where put IV overwhelms call IV at extreme percentiles, detecting unusual institutional put demand that may precede a directional move.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $HON
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1HON ↑80●● Strong$230.92 +2.10%10.2935.5%82BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No formal backtest exists to compare against, so the 90-day live sample of 9 trades is all there is: the headline 55.56% win rate sounds adequate but the -5.70% average 20-day return against SPY at +0.77% flags a fat left tail, confirmed by the June 2026 cohort going 0-for-2 at -24.48% average, and the screener remains in watch status with the monthly trend deteriorating into summer.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects put/call skew spikes above the 90-day baseline as a contrarian long signal when the underlying price action stays calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $NVDA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1NVDA ↓100●●● Very strong$206.25 +1.46%105%9—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No validated backtest exists yet (10 resolved trades versus the 30 required), so the 90-day live tape is the only scorecard, and while the top-line 51.4% WR and +4.7% avg beat SPY by 3.5 points supporting KEEP, the June cohort at -7.1% average and the tier inversion - Very Strong averaging -8.1% versus Watch at +10.2% - are the two numbers that most complicate that verdict and suggest the screener's own confidence grades are working backwards.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects failed breakouts at value area boundaries - one-day close outside reclaimed next session - forcing trapped shorts or longs to cover.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $APA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1APA ↑70● Signal$35.62 +2.25%BEAR_TRAP34.0836.831.23
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d live tape, the only performance reference available since no formal backtest exists, posts WR 66% and avg +5.1% beating a near-flat SPY by 5.1 points, but the monthly trend softens from May (77% WR, avg +8.0%) to June (63%, avg +4.4%), confirming the edge is real while flagging that it is decelerating.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 64% · avg +4.8% · beats SPY +0.0% by +4.8 pts
Last 90 days: 115 signals captured · 70 resolved at +20d · WR 64% · avg +4.8% · vs SPY +0.0%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 93 closed · WR TB 44% · 16% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Screens US equities for financial distress risk via Altman Z-score methodology, flagging balance-sheet deterioration that historically precedes credit events.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags institutional absorption and distribution by reading price spread relative to volume, isolating spots where smart money is likely active.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards

Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
